permitted_uses.conditions: The following uses are permitted in the RN Zone:
The following uses are permitted in the RN Zone:
The following uses are permitted in the RN Zone:
The following uses are permitted in the RN Zone:

provisions.text: The RN zone is intended to permit single-detached and semi-detached dwellings up to 4 units per lot (with conditions) and complimentary forms of uses that maintain community residential character (accessory dwelling uses, home occupations, home daycare, etc.).
Rezoning from RN to RM may be considered where the policies of the Official Plan can be demonstrated, otherwise, rezonings are not permitted in the RN Zone.
The following uses are permitted in the RN Zone:
Single Detached Dwelling (up to 4 units)
Semi Detached or Duplex Dwelling
Government Offices
Library
These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
Lot Area min. 318 m 2 for detached dwell in g, 240 m 2 for semi detached dwelling
Frontage min. 10.6 m, 8 m for semi detached dwelling
Front Yard Setback min. 6 m
Flankage Yard Setback min. 4 m
Side Yard Setback min. 1.8 m, 0 m for the shared wall of a semi detached dwelling
Rear Yard Setback min. 6 m
Coverage max. 40%
One or two attached ADU's may be permitted if the conditions in Section 4.5 can be met.
One rear-yard or side-yard detached Accessory Dwelling Units (ADU's) may be permitted if the conditions in Section 4.6 can be met.
Registered heritage buildings on lots that exceed 600 m 2 permit up to 6 attached ADU units if the conditions in Section 4.5 can be met.
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Home occupations are permitted if Section 4.8 requirements are met.
Home daycare uses are permitted if Section 4.9 requirements are met.
Notwithstanding Section 10.3.3, manufactured housing uses are permitted in the areas shown in Schedule 10.1 so long as the following requirements are met:
Lot Area min. 465 m 2 interior lot, 3,540 m 2 for corner lot
Frontage min. 12 m for interior lots, 15 m for corner lots
Front Yard Setback min. 6 m
Flankage Yard Setback min. 4 m
Side Yard Setback min. 1.8 m
Rear Yard Setback min. 4.5 m
Coverage max. 40%
One building per lot is permitted.
The maximum building height for the RN zone is 3 storeys.
Any garage (attached or detached) shall not project any closer to the streetline than the longest wall of the attached main dwelling that faces the same streetline.
Attached garages shall not exceed 50% of the primary building's frontage on any streetline to a maximum of 6 m in total.
Developers are encouraged to use materials and styles consistent with existing neighbourhood character.
To discourage excessive repetition of identical home designs within the same street block the following requirements must be met:
New residential development shall exhibit a variety of building designs along each block to reinforce individual identity and neighbourhood character.
The same dwelling design, including mirrored variations, shall not appear more than twice within any street block.
Developments proposing repeated models shall vary at least two of the following elements between dwellings:
Exterior cladding material;
Roof form or pitch;
Porch or entry design;
Window placement; or
Garage placement and style
All disturbed surfaces shall be seeded, sodded or hydroseeded with grass or meadow mixture or restored to natural condition.
All driveways shall be hard surfaced with asphalt, concrete or unit pavers. Loose gravel or soil is not permitted.
Parking in the front yard is only permitted on a hard surfaced driveways or parking spots.
No more than 40% of the front yard area can be driveway or parking spaces.
No less than 4 (min. 2 m high) trees shall be planted on each lot or 1 per manufactured home for units in Schedule 10.1 areas.
No less than 4 m 2 of shrub beds shall be planted in the front yard consisting of shrubs, annuals or perennials.

Where manufactured housing directly abuts the property line of any other housing type in the RN zone, a landscape buffer consisting of a minimum 1.8 m high opaque fence, and one tree (min. 2 m high) for every 8 m of abutting side or rear yard shall be installed. content_blocks.text: .3 Existing lots with frontage greater than 18 m shall not be subdivided except with an approved variance. .9 Neighbourhood local commercial is permitted in the RN Zone along any collector or arterial road as identified in Schedule C up to a gross floor area of 120 m 2 and by meeting the following requirements: (a) Three dedicated off-street automobile parking spaces and 2 class B biking parking spaces; and (b) A minimum 1.5 m wide hard surface sidewalk linking the front entry of the store with the parking area. (c) A landscape buffer 2 m wide shall be constructed which includes an opaque fence no less than 1.8 m high along all properties that directly abut the commercial use or any associated parking. Table 10.1 Minimum and Maximum As-of-Right Dimensions for the RN Zone Figure 10.1 RN Zone Built Form Standards Min. Side Yard Setback 1.8 m, 0 m for the shared wall of a Semi Max. Lot Coverage 35% Min. Lot Frontage 10.6 m, 8 m for Semi Min. Flanking Setback 4 m Min. Rear Yard Setback 6 m Min. Lot Area 318 m 2 , 240 m 2 for Semi Max. Building Height 3 Storeys (d) The Development Officer may approve alternative approaches that achieve an equivalent degree of architectural diversity. .7 To enhance streetscape interest and avoid flat wall surfaces, all street-facing facades shall include horizontal or vertical modulation of at least 150 mm in depth across no less than 20% of the facade area. .8 At least 4 m 2 of front porch (covered or uncovered) is required for every street facing facade.

open_issues.description: PDF text order and figure/table text were extracted with pypdf; verify section order, column flow, and page layout before normalization.
The zone contains table, figure, or schedule text. Values are preserved as extracted text and require source PDF layout review before normalization.
Some extracted text was not safely assigned to a labeled section and is preserved in content_blocks.
The source PDF places some dimensional and figure text before or beside related section headings in extracted text; verify provision assignment against the visual PDF.
